title: Stealing First Base

jokes.text: Dad, a meter is three inches longer than a yard... You have missed your turn.
jokes.setup: Lisa provides detailed mathematical conversion while dad ignores GPS
jokes.punchline: GPS announces they missed the turn while Lisa is still calculating
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa being overly academic while missing practical reality. Good character integration and timing.

jokes.text: That takes us into a construction site. Stupid kid thinks he's smarter than a computer.
jokes.setup: Bart correctly identifies GPS is directing them wrong
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses son's intelligence in favor of clearly malfunctioning technology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stubborness - trusting technology over his own kid's obvious correctness.

jokes.text: Decrease elevation ten feet, then turn left. D'oh!
jokes.setup: GPS gives impossible instruction to decrease elevation
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation implies he followed it and crashed
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of GPS malfunction joke, perfect Homer catchphrase usage, implies great physical gag.

jokes.text: Decrease elevation ten feet, then turn left. D'oh!
jokes.setup: GPS tells them to decrease elevation
jokes.punchline: Homer follows directions literally and drives off something
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of GPS malfunction with Homer's literal interpretation leading to physical comedy

jokes.text: Turn right in 2.3 kilometers... Why, you... Recalculating. So cold. So cold.
jokes.setup: GPS continues giving directions after apparent crash
jokes.punchline: Homer threatens the GPS, which then 'recalculates' while Homer suffers
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of GPS gag with Homer's suffering as payoff.

jokes.text: Mrs. Krabappel had to go to portland. The people she hired to deprogram her sister from that cult turned out to be an even worse cult.
jokes.setup: Teacher is absent for family emergency
jokes.punchline: Escalating cult situation - anti-cult group is worse cult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke - each detail makes situation more absurd. Great Simpsons-style background story.

jokes.text: They have music on tuesdays. Their teacher takes her shoes off. Their twins are one boy, one girl.
jokes.setup: Kids worried about differences with other fourth grade class
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of increasingly mundane 'differences' ending with basic biology
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from trivial to absurd concerns, captures kid logic perfectly.

jokes.text: Is anyone here? Oh, whatever. Is anyone here? Whatever. Is anyone here? Is anyone here? Whatever. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Overwhelmed teacher trying to take attendance
jokes.punchline: Rapid deterioration into giving up with repeated 'whatever'
jokes.explanation: Decent teacher burnout joke, captures the chaos of merged classes.

jokes.text: My parents took me to hawaii once, But I was intimidated By the physiques of the local kids, So I just stayed in the hotel room.
jokes.setup: New kid asks if Bart surfs
jokes.punchline: Bart's pathetically honest confession about Hawaii trip
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart insecurity moment - surprisingly vulnerable and specific.

jokes.text: That's cool. No, it's not cool.
jokes.setup: New kid tries to be supportive
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately corrects him with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - Bart's self-awareness makes the cringe funnier.

jokes.text: You are so dead. Sorry. Let me get that for you. you're blind.
jokes.setup: Bully threatens new student then helps pick up dropped items
jokes.punchline: Reveals the student is blind, explaining the help
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection about bully's motivations.

jokes.text: If anyone messes with this kid, I will destroy them. It's okay. No one's messing with me. You're not a freak, And I won't let you think that about yourself. I don't think I'm a freak. So brave.
jokes.setup: Nelson becomes protective of blind student
jokes.punchline: Blind kid keeps correcting Nelson's assumptions while Nelson ignores him
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Nelson's misguided protectiveness versus kid's actual confidence.

jokes.text: Oh, look at you-- Opening a book so you won't have to talk to me. Well, I'm just going to be tuning up the old armpit.
jokes.setup: Girl ignores Bart by reading
jokes.punchline: Bart threatens armpit fart noises as retaliation
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart immaturity - 'tuning up the old armpit' is a great phrase.

jokes.text: Lisa simpson, f. An f?! Okay, this is the time when I always wake up. Come on, alarm clock, get me out of this.
jokes.setup: Lisa gets an F grade
jokes.punchline: Lisa assumes it must be a nightmare since she never fails
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her identity so tied to grades that failure seems impossible.

jokes.text: Sorry, lisa-- this f is all too real. No it's not, dream hoover. And as long as I'm asleep, I'm going to have some fun. I can fly! Wheeee! This isn't a dream. I really did fail.
jokes.setup: Lisa still thinks she's dreaming
jokes.punchline: She tries to fly to prove it's a dream and presumably crashes
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Lisa's denial into physical comedy attempt.

jokes.text: Little miss perfect. Whoa. Damn, this caterpillar can eat. damn. Damn! Do you mind?
jokes.setup: Students mock Lisa's perfect image after her failure
jokes.punchline: Lisa's swearing escalates as she embraces her bad girl image
jokes.explanation: Good character development joke - Lisa discovering rebellion through mild swearing.

jokes.text: Oh, man, you girls ruin everything- even vampires.
jokes.setup: Girls interrupt boys' vampire discussion
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated complaint that girls ruin vampires
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about gender dynamics in kid culture around vampire obsession.

jokes.text: And they don't put product in their hair Like this loser. They look like this. [Shows drawing]
jokes.setup: Student explains what real vampires look like
jokes.punchline: Shows drawing that's presumably ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Visual gag dependent on animation - likely a crude drawing for humor.

jokes.text: Can I draw a robot barfing screws On your arm with my sharpie? I... I'd like that.
jokes.setup: Student offers to draw on someone's arm
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd image of robot vomiting hardware, eager acceptance
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific absurd image that's both gross and mechanical - great kid logic.

jokes.text: An f. An f! I hope harvard never finds out about this.
jokes.setup: Lisa still processing her F grade
jokes.punchline: Worries about college admission implications in elementary school
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa overthinking - worrying about Harvard in elementary school.

jokes.text: Ever since you got that bad grade, You seem a lot cooler. Lisa, you want to come skip rope? You can replace the tree.
jokes.setup: Lisa's failure makes her popular
jokes.punchline: Offered to replace an inanimate object in jump rope
jokes.explanation: Good irony about how failure makes Lisa relatable, plus absurd rope detail.

jokes.text: There was a mix-up with your test. you got An a-triple-plus. Seems the f Belonged to ralph. I cheated wrong. I copied the lisa name and used the ralph answers.
jokes.setup: Test mix-up revealed
jokes.punchline: Ralph's backwards logic about cheating - copied wrong person's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph logic - cheating wrong by copying the smart kid's NAME but using his own terrible answers. Brilliant reversal.

jokes.text: Gifted! Gifted! Gifted! I'm just advanced. You can catch up. Gifted!
jokes.setup: Students taunting Lisa about being in gifted class
jokes.punchline: Lisa's condescending attempt to be humble backfires
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment - her attempt at humility comes across as more condescending.

jokes.text: your mother thinks I'm working on the car. But I need to talk about man stuff. Talk to grampa. He used to be a man. He did?
jokes.setup: Bart needs advice about girls
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of Grampa's masculinity, Bart's surprised reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer casual cruelty and Bart's innocent shock at the implication.

jokes.text: There's one sure way to find out If a girl likes you-- steal a kiss. Really? Did that ever work for you? Sure did. I remember it like it was yesterday.
jokes.setup: Grampa gives dating advice about stealing kisses
jokes.punchline: Implies his 'success' was decades ago and probably inappropriate by today's standards
jokes.explanation: Good setup for bad advice from different era - classic generation gap humor.

jokes.text: Kiss her, ya idjit!
jokes.setup: Bart hesitating about kissing the girl
jokes.punchline: Grampa's impatient encouragement with his signature insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to advice scene with classic Grampa vocabulary.

jokes.text: Say, 'haw-haw.' Haw-haw. That was great! Really great!
jokes.setup: Nelson coaching someone to mock Bart
jokes.punchline: Nelson's enthusiastic approval of the mocking performance
jokes.explanation: Classic Nelson bullying behavior with enthusiastic coaching element.

jokes.text: Earlier today, your son engaged in inappropriate Mouth-on-mouth contact with our daughter. You mean, he kissed her? And I got to miss work?!
jokes.setup: Parents using legal language for simple kiss
jokes.punchline: Homer's disappointment that he left work for something so minor
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between overwrought legal parents and Homer's work priorities.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you guys should look for easier jobs.
jokes.setup: Lawyer parents being difficult and demanding
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple solution to their stress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer simplicity - missing the point entirely with logical suggestion.

jokes.text: And we will make you regret the day Your son was born. You're halfway home, lady.
jokes.setup: Mother threatens to make Homer regret Bart's birth
jokes.punchline: Homer agrees he already regrets it halfway
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Homer casually admitting he already regrets having Bart. Perfect economy and shock value.

jokes.text: You mean second base? Whoa, he's a little young for that.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to club girl and drag to cave
jokes.punchline: Bart misinterprets as sexual base terminology, Homer's concern about age
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with baseball terminology confusion and Homer's priorities.

jokes.text: We'll be sued into the poor house And have to eat garbage forever. That's no problem. Mom can make garbage taste great. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Homer warns about eating garbage if sued
jokes.punchline: Bart's confidence in Marge's cooking skills, her pleased reaction
jokes.explanation: Sweet family dynamic with Bart's faith in Marge and her appreciation.

jokes.text: If only real life was in 3-d. and the glasses smell Like the last guy who wore them had pizza.
jokes.setup: Father-son bonding at 3D movie
jokes.punchline: Gross reality of shared 3D glasses
jokes.explanation: Relatable gross-out humor about movie theater hygiene.

jokes.text: With mulch love-- lisa simpson. mulch love. That's cute.
jokes.setup: Lisa signing off gardening blog post
jokes.punchline: Garden-themed pun on 'much love'
jokes.explanation: Cute pun that fits Lisa's gardening interest and nerdy personality.

jokes.text: Flotus1? Who could that be? Skinner : Attention, all students. The third grade's performance of my fair lady is cancelled. I say, that's rum news. Very rum, indeed.
jokes.setup: School announcement about cancelled play
jokes.punchline: Third graders staying in character with British accents
jokes.explanation: Good kid theater humor - staying in character even when disappointed.

jokes.text: Oh! I finally caught one of bart's school plays. That's not bart. It's principal skinner. Bart's sitting right next to you. Yeah, now that I look closer, That guy couldn't fool anybody.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks Skinner is Bart in school play
jokes.punchline: Doubles down on obvious mistake even after correction
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - refuses to admit obvious error.

jokes.text: Shut up, fatso. Why you little...
jokes.setup: Bart insults Homer
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer strangling response
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons callback but predictable - gets points for being iconic.

jokes.text: Hey, willie, does he taste like failure?
jokes.setup: Willie being forced to kiss in demonstration
jokes.punchline: Student's cruel question about the taste
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from kids about Willie's unfortunate situation.

jokes.text: I can make these two kiss all day, if I have to. If you want them to keep on kissing, just keep laughing.
jokes.setup: Assembly is backfiring with students laughing
jokes.punchline: Skinner's threat backfires by encouraging more laughter
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Skinner's discipline strategy completely backfires.

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? I'm kissing you, stupid. You're the biggest psycho I ever met. You want to stop? Absolutely not.
jokes.setup: Unexpected kiss between main characters
jokes.punchline: Girl insults boy while continuing to kiss him
jokes.explanation: Great relationship comedy - attraction despite antagonism.

jokes.text: A vh-60n whitehawk!
jokes.setup: Helicopter arrival at school
jokes.punchline: Overly specific military identification of aircraft
jokes.explanation: Mild military nerd humor with specific aircraft knowledge.

jokes.text: I wanted just 'flotus,' but someone had it. That's me, 'cause I swim with my flotuses on.
jokes.setup: Explaining her online username
jokes.punchline: Kid's misunderstanding about flotation devices
jokes.explanation: Good pun confusion between FLOTUS and flotation devices.

jokes.text: I'll tell them what the lesson is. He's our joe biden.
jokes.setup: Skinner interrupting First Lady's speech
jokes.punchline: Obama's diplomatic comparison to Vice President
jokes.explanation: Clever political humor comparing Skinner's interruptions to Biden's tendencies.

jokes.text: Fifth in my class at annapolis. Scaled the summit of the highest peaks On six of seven continents. Hey, that's great. Don't forget to bring the scooper When you walk the dog.
jokes.setup: Military character listing impressive achievements
jokes.punchline: Obama reduces him to basic pet care duties
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of military bragging with mundane dog-walking reminder.

jokes.text: Sorry, ma'am. We can't seem to reopen the hatch to the copter. You were saying? Oh, well, I loosened it up.
jokes.setup: Military can't open helicopter door
jokes.punchline: First Lady takes credit after they figure it out
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about taking credit for others' work.

jokes.text: I want you to act the same way two days in a row! Ugh! I'm not gonna let you hurt me anymore.
jokes.setup: Couple arguing about relationship expectations
jokes.punchline: Girl's frustrated demand for consistency
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor about consistency expectations.

jokes.text: He's dead! I love him! Oh, he's breathing. Loser.
jokes.setup: Girl thinks Bart is dead and confesses love
jokes.punchline: Immediately calls him loser when he's alive
jokes.explanation: Excellent emotional whiplash from romantic to cruel in seconds.

jokes.text: Who knows gob-to-gob?! You know, mutton hole to mutton hole. Any physical contact violates our school's no-touch policy.
jokes.setup: Willie asking for CPR help with crude terminology
jokes.punchline: Skinner's bureaucratic response preventing life-saving
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Willie's crude concern and Skinner's bureaucracy.

jokes.text: Let's quickly prod him to the curb with this pool skimmer.
jokes.setup: Can't touch unconscious student due to policy
jokes.punchline: Treating child like pool debris
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny bureaucratic solution - treating student like pool maintenance.

jokes.text: I prefer a dead child to a lawsuit from your parents.
jokes.setup: Nikki wants to help unconscious Bart
jokes.punchline: Skinner's brutal admission about priorities
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest dark comedy about institutional priorities - shocking and perfectly in character for Skinner.

jokes.text: Nelson, now that the ban's been lifted, Let me touch your face. That way, I can see you with my fingers. Ew! I said your face, not your butt!
jokes.setup: Blind student wants to feel Nelson's face
jokes.punchline: Nelson presents wrong body part
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection humor with Nelson's crude interpretation.

jokes.text: Oh, you feel comfortable enough to mock me. That's for mocking me. Nelson, your punch restored my vision! I can see again!
jokes.setup: Blind student mocks Nelson
jokes.punchline: Punch miraculously cures blindness
jokes.explanation: Absurd miracle cure through violence - good Simpsons cartoon logic.

jokes.text: Really? Kevin, that's wonder... The student has become the master!
jokes.setup: Nelson amazed at miracle cure
jokes.punchline: Immediately punches the newly sighted student
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson behavior - amazement followed by immediate violence.

jokes.text: Oh, bart, you've got a few things To learn about women. And I will never tell you what they are.
jokes.setup: Bart confused by girl's mixed signals
jokes.punchline: Girl acknowledges his confusion but refuses to help
jokes.explanation: Perfect relationship humor about the impossibility of understanding women.

jokes.text: I give up. Smell ya later. I love you!
jokes.setup: Bart gives up and walks away
jokes.punchline: Girl immediately declares love once he's leaving
jokes.explanation: Classic relationship timing humor - wanting what you can't have.

tmdb_overview: Principal Skinner announces that Mrs. Krabappel was called out of town and budget cuts dictate that, until she returns, the school's two fourth grade classes will merge. Bart reluctantly shares a desk with Nikki and develops a flirtatious rapport. Bart talks to Grampa about his new crush, and at Grampa's suggestion, gives Nikki a kiss. But when Nikki starts sending Bart mixed signals, he swears off women forever. Meanwhile, Lisa's classmates ostracize her for being an overachiever, and First Lady Michelle Obama, a self-professed nerd, comes to Lisa's defence.
